search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae cough telehealth clinical guideline 2024 PDF
site:brit-thoracic.org.uk chronic cough adults clinical statement 2023
site:nice.org.uk NG12 cough smoker haemoptysis recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Treatment trials do not establish generic documentation support.
Medicine names and doses were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: DHA does not state an age boundary in applicability, while the BTS statement is adult-only and the NICE recommendations have age and smoking qualifiers.
setting_applicability: Partial: direct for DHA telehealth cough documentation and adult chronic-cough assessment, with selected referral and suspected-cancer recognition contexts.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth fields; BTS and NICE supporting evidence requires UAE clinician review and local cancer and referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 cough guideline, BTS 2023 statement, and NICE NG12 recommendations updated 2026-04-15 were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA issue or BTS statement was identified; NG12 remains current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped cough presentation scope within DHA telehealth.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped haemoptysis, weight loss, fever or night sweats, and assessed-negative fields as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped duration, character, sputum, timing, triggers, reflux or postnasal context, medicine and smoking history, and structured history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Was reviewed for investigation context; the more specific mapped chest-radiograph and spirometry fields use the BTS statement.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.
Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult chronic-cough identity and matching terms.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines chronic cough in adults as cough lasting more than 8 weeks and sets the statement scope.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped chest, oropharyngeal, and nasal examination fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped chest radiography, spirometry, selected upper-airway referral, and clinician referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports the mapped age-and-smoking-qualified new-cough warning as a prompt only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

unresolved_source_gaps: The legacy workflow does not enforce the adult, more-than-8-week, age, smoking, or telehealth qualifiers of the exact sources.

Voice change or stridor, persistent localized abnormality, oxygen saturation, respiratory rate, previous-investigation review, generic clinician plans, inhaled-therapy trials, fixed follow-up, PRN wording, and some generic safety-netting remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, cancer-risk interpretation, investigation selection, treatment, urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
